audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for opening, God of all comfort, affliction/pressure, death sentence in Asia, prayer help, sincerity, changed travel plans, Yes in Messiah, Spirit seal/pledge.

verses.restored: Paul, apostle of Messiah Jesus through the will of the Divine, and Timothy the sibling, to the assembly of the Divine that is in Corinth, with all the holy ones who are in all Achaia:
Grace to you and peace from Divine our Father and Lord Jesus Messiah.
Blessed is the Divine and Father of our Lord Jesus Messiah, the Father of compassions and Divine of all comfort/encouragement,

the One comforting/encouraging us in all our pressure, so that we may be able to comfort/encourage those in every pressure through the comfort/encouragement with which we ourselves are comforted/encouraged by the Divine.
Because just as the sufferings of Messiah abound into us, so through Messiah also our comfort/encouragement abounds.

But whether we are pressured, it is for your comfort/encouragement and salvation/healing; whether we are comforted/encouraged, it is for your comfort/encouragement, working in endurance of the same sufferings that we also suffer.
And our hope for you is firm, knowing that as you are sharers of the sufferings, so also of the comfort/encouragement.
For we do not want you to be unaware, siblings, concerning our pressure that happened in Asia, that we were weighed down beyond measure, beyond strength, so that we despaired even of living.
But we ourselves have had the sentence of death in ourselves, so that we would not trust upon ourselves but upon the Divine who raises the dead.
Who rescued us from so great a death and will rescue, in whom we have hoped that he will still rescue,
you also helping together by prayer for us, so that from many faces thanks may be given on our behalf for the grace-gift given to us through many.

For our boasting is this: the witness of our conscience, that in simplicity/sincerity and purity of the Divine, not in fleshly wisdom but in grace of the Divine, we conducted ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.
For we write no other things to you than what you read or also understand; and I hope you will understand to the end,
just as you also understood us in part, that we are your boast, as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.
And in this confidence I was intending to come to you first, so that you might have a second grace,
and through you to pass into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you and by you to be sent forward to Judea.
Therefore, intending this, did I then use lightness? Or what I plan, do I plan according to flesh, so that with me there should be Yes, yes and No, no?
But trustworthy is the Divine, that our word toward you is not Yes and No.
For the Son of the Divine, Jesus Messiah, who was proclaimed among you through us, through me and Silvanus and Timothy, did not become Yes and No, but in him Yes has come to be.
For as many as are promises of the Divine, in him is the Yes; therefore also through him is the Amen to the Divine for glory through us.
But the One confirming us with you into Messiah and anointing us is the Divine,
who also sealed us and gave the pledge/down payment of the Spirit in our hearts.
But I call the Divine as witness upon my soul/life, that to spare you I no longer came to Corinth.
Not that we lord over your trust, but we are coworkers of your joy; for by trust you stand.

translation_notes: Paraklesis and parakaleo are intentionally rendered comfort/encouragement to preserve the repeated word family.
Paul's leadership posture in 1:24 is non-domineering: not lording over trust, but coworkers of joy.
